channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical marks an unprecedented alliance between the Church and Silicon Valley.

item.title: The Best Movies to Stream This Month (May 2026)
item.description: Bugonia, Good Boy, and Send Help are among the films deserving of your eyeballs this month.

item.title: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
item.description: In Magnifica Humanitas, the Pope decries the concentration of technological power in a few global players.

item.title: Every organization is pouring money into AI right now, and almost none of them know what their people are actually doing with it': Study reveals employees are using their personal AI accounts at work, raising a whole host of issues
item.description: Shadow AI exposes company data and risks IP loss – but personal tools offer easier access than clunky enterprise accounts.

item.title: American Air inks deal with Starlink for fastest Wi-Fi in the sky
item.description: The world's largest airline inks a deal with Elon Musk's internet company.

item.title: How to watch Crystal Palace vs. Rayo Vallecano online for free
item.description: How to watch Conference League for free. Live stream Crystal Palace vs. Rayo Vallecano in the Conference League final for free.

item.title: Why people are losing it over Ferrari’s first all-electric car
item.description: Initial reaction to the Jony Ive-designed Ferrari Luce is that the EV doesn’t look much like a Ferrari at all. Ferrari’s first-ever all-electric vehicle, the Ferrari Luce, was designed to look “entirely new,” the company says, and so far the reaction to the new EV has been polarized.

item.title: ‘Anti-tech extremism’: The government is monitoring AI criticism nationwide, says report
item.description: Unpublished documents from federal intelligence agencies and local law enforcement reveal a new term for anti-AI sentiment. Being critical of AI is far from a fringe position in the United States. Recent polling shows that half of U.S. adults feel more concerned than excited about the increased use of AI in daily life, and among Gen Z specifically, excitement and hope around AI are falling while anger over the tech increases, with 42% of Gen Zers saying AI makes them anxious....

item.title: I found 11 surprise Memorial Day Apple deals that are still live: Grab M5 MacBook Airs for $899, AirPods for $99
item.description: Looking for the best Memorial Day deals on Apple products? You can score the M5 MacBook Air for $899 and the AirPods 4 for $99.

item.title: Microsoft's unexpected MacBook Neo challenger might be a budget laptop you've never heard of before
item.description: The Chuwi UniBook is one of the first Intel Wildcat Lake laptops to leverage Intel's Core 3 304 CPU, even as it undercuts Apple's competing MacBook Neo by $150.

item.title: Hackers claim to be selling 340 million stolen OnlyFans records — but experts are already skeptical on how serious hack is
item.description: OnlyFans says the claims are false, so users can breathe easy.

item.title: The attack dominating financial services doesn't steal passwords. It resets MFA and steals the token.
item.description: The attacker who hit the most financial services organizations over the past 12 months never phished a password. They called an IT support line, convinced an employee to reset their MFA, and registered their own device on the network.CrowdStrike’s 2026 Financial Services Threat Landscape Report, released this month and covering activity from April 2025 through March 2026, identified Mutant Spider as the single most active threat to the financial services sector. The group’s primary technique was voice phishing over Microsoft Teams. Operators impersonated internal IT support, convinced employee...

item.title: I just watched a Chinese robot make the worst eggs I've ever seen and I'm a little less worried about robots taking our jobs
item.description: GigaAI's SeeLight S1 does laundry, folds clothes, handles a microwave, but its egg-making skills are a bit iffy.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang calls AI a “lazy” excuse for layoffs
item.description: In an interview with Channel News Asia, Huang gave his advice to people who are worried about losing their jobs to AI. Jensen Huang has some pointed words for leaders who blame company layoffs on AI.

item.title: Spotify CEO says putting AI-generated music on the app is good—and not just for SPOT stock. Here’s why
item.description: Instead of curbing AI music slop, Alex Norström is doubling down on it. We’ve written a lot about how AI is coming for your job. Now AI is coming for your music, flooding streaming platforms with “AI music slop.” But instead of curbing it, Spotify’s CEO Alex Norström is doubling down and embracing AI-generated music—claiming it offers artists protection from piracy, and music-lovers more freedom to listen to and create more of the kind of music they want....

item.title: Bosgame P4 Ultra review: Nothing here convinces me that AMD Zen 3 is sufficiently exciting for the current mini PC market
item.description: The Bosgame P4 Ultra uses an older AMD Ryzen 7 architecture but remains an effective general-purpose mini PC

item.title: The 4.5-star-rated Google Pixel Watch just fell back to its Black Friday price on Amazon
item.description: The Google Pixel Watch 4 is one of the best Android smartwatches you can buy today, and this 4.5-star-rated wearable is now back to its Black Friday price on Amazon.

item.title: 4 great tools to produce your own PDF bookazine
item.description: How to turn your top content into a bookazine – or get AI to do it for you.

item.title: Et si le Big Bang avait créé deux Univers ? Une nouvelle théorie remet en question tout ce que nous savons
item.description: Dans une hypothèse aussi vertigineuse que fascinante, le physicien Naman Kumar, de l’Institut indien de technologie de Gandhinagar, propose une nouvelle approche du Big Bang, qui aurait pu être un évènement double.

item.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
item.description: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google Health, as previously announced, and many of the responses we've seen so far are full of confusion, frustration, […]

item.title: ‘They choose not to buy because they want to develop their own’: China just refused to buy a single Nvidia AI chip as Trump’s plan backfires
item.description: China has refused to buy Nvidia’s H200 AI chips despite personal intervention from Donald Trump.

item.title: Uber and Lyft drivers in Massachusetts certify the nation’s first ride-hailing union
item.description: Supporters say rising vehicle costs, fluctuating pay, and opaque app algorithms have fueled frustration among drivers. Drivers for ride-hailing apps such as Uber and Lyft in Massachusetts became the first in the nation Tuesday to certify a union, marking a milestone in the growing effort to organize gig-economy workers amid ongoing concerns over pay, expenses, and working conditions....
